label: Arcech-Sylandris
description: Arcech-Sylandris rises as a vertical labyrinth of power—a sprawling metropolis that defines itself not by districts but by altitude. The official seat of the planetary council, this capital is where power brokers, merchants, entertainers, and politicians negotiate fate across eight dimensional vectors of infrastructure. Old money dwells in the high spires; ambitious enterprises claw through every level seeking legitimacy. Ground level is a mere concept here.

Population: ~850 million residents stratified across vertical zones. The highest wealth concentration on Arcech is inextricably linked to elevation—penthouse power versus Roots desperation. Species diversity and class-based segregation rigidly enforce the skyward hierarchy. The Aether-Fog obscures from below, creating a false sky for those in The Roots; for those in the spires, it is just a shimmering curtain beneath their feet.

Geography: A three-dimensional urban sprawl built on 8D infrastructure. The Roots—lower canyons where Aether-Fog pools thick and neon—house commercial zones, artisan workshops, and service workers on moss-covered stone stairs and pillar faces. Mid-level platforms accommodate residential tenements pierced by maglev rail networks that shriek between weathered walls. Upper spires hold palatial estates, government sanctums, and cultural power-centers; here, ancient enchanted masonry meets gleaming Armanitech alloys and holoscreens. An orbital platform floats above, secondary to Starfall, yet still prestigious.

Economy: Banking, real estate (vertical property rights are complex), politics, and culture drive the city's flow. House Valorian controls the most valuable altitude zones and finance pipelines threading through the vertical infrastructure. Armanitech maintains corporate offices across multiple levels, leveraging gravity-wells for manufacturing. Currency flows upward; broken promises filter down. Imports luxury goods and artisan materials; exports banking services, cultural cachet, and political influence.

Culture: Status is measured in altitude and air-quality. Power derives from ancestry, wealth, education, and connections—especially vertical connectivity via maglev access. Legitimate authority and shadow networks interpenetrate seamlessly. Public propriety—gleaming facades, holographic propaganda, refined etiquette—masks private cutthroat competition. Ozone and rain-damp mystique cling to ancient stones; holographic neon pulses against centuries-old enchanted masonry. The city oppresses through beauty and grandeur alike.

Dominant Power: House Valorian (banking dynasty controlling premium altitude real estate and financial conduits). Other presences: Armanitech (corporate platforms and government liaison), Concordat Trading House (secondary embassy on mid-levels), Velvet Mask (shadow networks operating across all vertical strata), Exodian Church (growing foothold in lower tenements), Brug's Gobbos (everywhere, occupying the gaps in every dimension).

containsPlace.label: The Palace Quarter
containsPlace.description: The highest spires of Arcech-Sylandris—a realm of breathtaking altitude where palatial estates and administrative sanctums float above the Aether-Fog's false sky. Home of House Valorian and the planetary council, these towers are carved from ancient enchanted stone yet bristle with Armanitech alloys and holoscreens projecting power and wealth. From these crystalline heights, the lower city appears as a neon-fogged well of light impossibly far below. Guarded by layers of security, gatekeeping not just access but breathable air itself. The formal seat of all legitimate authority—though the view from here makes shadow networks seem like quaint distractions.

containsPlace.label: The Commerce Ring
containsPlace.description: Suspended across mid-altitude platforms, the Commerce Ring is where vertical currency flows and investments are made across eight dimensions of infrastructure. Gleaming Armanitech trading towers interspersed with weathered stone facades house banking operations, exchange bureaus, and financial power-brokers. Maglev rail networks shriek through market plazas cut into pillar-faces; holoscreens cascade down ancient masonry walls showing live feeds of capital movement. The Aether-Fog pools around the lower edges, creating a visual barrier between traders and the Roots below. Heavily secured at every level—both by guards and by the architecture itself, which privileges high-altitude access.

containsPlace.label: The Cultural Mile
containsPlace.description: A vertical ribbon of intellectual grandeur straddling upper-mid altitude, where museums, galleries, theaters, and academies occupy terraced levels carved into support pillars and building faces. Here, ancient enchanted stone holds neoclassical sculptures alongside holographic art installations that flicker with ozone-laced air. The smell of rain-damp masonry mingles with the hum of cutting-edge projection systems. High society conducts its sophisticated displays from climate-controlled galleries looking down onto the Aether-Fog's shimmer. Yet beneath this cultural veneer, the vertical architecture oppresses: the Roots lurk far below, visible only as a neon ribbon, if at all. Intellectual authority is a ladder only some are permitted to climb.

containsPlace.label: The Common Markets
containsPlace.description: Deep within the Aether-Fog's thick embrace lie the Roots' vital artisan and service sectors. Market stalls cling to the vertical faces of massive support pillars, their awnings sagging under centuries of damp weight. Craftspeople work on moss-covered stone stairs that wind through canyons of tenement walls. Merchant voices echo upward through narrow sky-wells where the Aether-Fog glows faint neon far, far above—not a sky but a ceiling. Commerce here is essential and contemned in equal measure: commoners conduct vital business while elites call it a ghetto. Maglev rail-shadows cross overhead; the thunder of trains on upper tracks rattles workshop walls and vendor stalls continually. This is where the city's wealth is built; few above acknowledge it.

containsPlace.label: The Twilight Districts
containsPlace.description: Shadow-work hubs honeycombed between vertical levels, where legitimate businesses and black-market operations are separated only by maglev routes and ancient masonry thresholds. In the mist-veiled mid-zones, contraband flows through pillar-tunnels while holographic covers display respectable facades to upper-level monitoring systems. Here, Velvet Mask operates freely—they understand the geometry of shadow better than anyone. The Aether-Fog provides natural cover; the vertical architecture provides plausible deniability (deny which level?). Epicenter of shadow economy and political intrigue, where both ozone-scented ancient stone and cutting-edge Armanitech are leveraged for subterfuge. The city's legitimate authority does not look here—by design, and because looking down from the spires is easier than looking sideways.
